title: Radius of Curvature Measurements for Osteochondral Allograft Location
author: Zachary Goldstein
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.9nmh5c6
materials: Use area to derive radius of curvature.
Clear all measurements on sagittal slice.
Determining Radius of Curvature of the Capitellum

steps.title: Selecting a Patient & Formatting a
steps.instruction: Selecting a Patient & Formatting a. Obtain all CT images from the Department of Radiology tagged "upper extremity" or "elbow" and "lower extremity" or "knee". b. Review lists and exclude patients with indication of prior intra-articular fracture, surgery, degeneration, or variations of normal anatomy. c. Randomize list, and select first patient for measurement. d. Remove the demographics toggle from the screen, and select screen format (Series 1:2). e. Display axial and sagittal views, set image contrast to "Bone". f. Along the axial image, begin at the inferior-most point. Scroll superiorly to identify the axial slice that displays the longest distance between the anterior and posterior points (APL = Anterior Posterior Length) of the subchondral bone of both the medial and lateral condyles. NOTE: All measurements in this study were evaluated using a picture archiving and communications system (IMPAX, Agfa Healthcare, Mortsel, BE). In this study, scans of patients for upper extremity measurements were limited between 11 and 21 years.

steps.title: Determining Radius of Curvature of the Medial Femoral Condyle

steps.title: Determining Radius of Curvature of the Lateral Femoral Condyle
steps.instruction: Determining Radius of Curvature of the Lateral Femoral Condyle

steps.title: On the sagittal view, begin medially...
steps.instruction: On the sagittal view, begin medially and scroll laterally until the sagittal slice aligns with the posterior endpoint of the APL of the medial condyle. If the sagittal slice does not align with the endpoint, choose the sagittal slice medial to the endpoint.

steps.title: Switch to the axial view so...
steps.instruction: Switch to the axial view so that the tracked line is visible on the sagittal view. Draw a line on the sagittal view that traces this line.

steps.title: Measure 15 mm inferior and perpendicular to this line
steps.instruction: Measure 15 mm inferior and perpendicular to this line.

steps.title: Draw a line measuring 10mm superior...
steps.instruction: Draw a line measuring 10mm superior from the inferior endpoint of the 15mm measurement.

steps.title: Draw two perpendicular lines extending through...
steps.instruction: Draw two perpendicular lines extending through the condylar bone at the endpoints of the two lines.

steps.title: Erase all measurement lines drawn on...
steps.instruction: Erase all measurement lines drawn on the sagittal slice EXCEPT for the two perpendicular lines drawn.

steps.title: Use the "circle" function to trace...
steps.instruction: Use the "circle" function to trace a best-fit circle around the radius of the subchondral bone in between the two perpendicular lines. g

steps.title: Record the area and perimeter
steps.instruction: Record the area and perimeter.

steps.title: Identify the superior notch of the...
steps.instruction: Identify the superior notch of the posterior aspect of the condyle and measure 20mm down from that point.

steps.title: Draw a line that measures 10mm...
steps.instruction: Draw a line that measures 10mm superior from the inferior endpoint of the 20mm line.

steps.title: Draw two perpendicular lines at the...
steps.instruction: Draw two perpendicular lines at the endpoints of the two measurements through the posterior subchondral bone.

steps.title: Erase all measurements on the sagittal...
steps.instruction: Erase all measurements on the sagittal slice EXCEPT for the two perpendicular lines drawn.

steps.title: Draw a circle that best fits...
steps.instruction: Draw a circle that best fits the radius of curvature of the sub-chondral bone between the two perpendicular lines.

steps.title: Record the area and perimeter
steps.instruction: Record the area and perimeter.

steps.title: On the sagittal view, begin medial...
steps.instruction: On the sagittal view, begin medial and scroll lateral until the sagittal slice aligns with the posterior endpoint of the APL measurement line of the lateral condyle. If the sagittal slice does not align with the endpoint, choose the sagittal slice medial to the endpoint.

steps.title: Switch to the axial view so...
steps.instruction: Switch to the axial view so that the tracked line is visible on the sagittal view. Draw a line on the sagittal view that traces this line.

steps.title: Measure 15mm inferior and perpendicular to this line
steps.instruction: Measure 15mm inferior and perpendicular to this line.

steps.title: Measure 10mm superior from the inferior...
steps.instruction: Measure 10mm superior from the inferior endpoint of the 15mm measurement.

steps.title: Draw two perpendicular lines at the...
steps.instruction: Draw two perpendicular lines at the endpoints of the two measurements extending through the posterior subchondral bone.

steps.title: Erase all measurement lines drawn on...
steps.instruction: Erase all measurement lines drawn on the sagittal slice EXCEPT for the perpendicular lines drawn.

steps.title: Draw a circle that best fits...
steps.instruction: Draw a circle that best fits the radius of curvature of the subchondral bone in between the two lines.

steps.title: Record the area and perimeter
steps.instruction: Record the area and perimeter.

steps.title: Clear all lines and measurements on...
steps.instruction: Clear all lines and measurements on the sagittal slice and remain on same sagittal slice.

steps.title: Identify the superior notch of the...
steps.instruction: Identify the superior notch of the posterior condyle and Measure 20mm down.

steps.title: Measure 10mm superior from the inferior...
steps.instruction: Measure 10mm superior from the inferior endpoint of the 20mm line

steps.title: Draw two perpendicular lines at the...
steps.instruction: Draw two perpendicular lines at the endpoints of the two measurements extending through the subchondral bone.

steps.title: Erase all measurement lines drawn on...
steps.instruction: Erase all measurement lines drawn on the sagittal slice EXCEPT for the perpendicular lines drawn.

steps.title: Draw a circle that best fits...
steps.instruction: Draw a circle that best fits the radius of curvature of the subchondral bone in between the two lines.

steps.title: Record the area and perimeter
steps.instruction: Record the area and perimeter.

steps.title: On the coronal image, identify the...
steps.instruction: On the coronal image, identify the capitellar articular cartilage bump and draw a 4mm line medially from that point.

steps.title: Scroll along the sagittal image until...
steps.instruction: Scroll along the sagittal image until the slice matches up with medial edge of the 4mm line. If the line does not match perfectly select the slice medial to the point.

steps.title: Draw a line through the capitellum...
steps.instruction: Draw a line through the capitellum bisecting the radial head at the midline. Draw a line through the capitellum from the superior side that approximates the shaft of the humerus. If the arm is angled at 90 degrees use the anterior aspect of the humerus.

steps.title: Draw a circle that best fits...
steps.instruction: Draw a circle that best fits the radius of curvature of the subchondral bone with the bisection of the lines as the center.

steps.title: Record the area and perimeter
steps.instruction: Record the area and perimeter.
